articles.title: From 'Starfield' to Artemis: designing moonbases in games and reality
articles.description: Looking at the best games for building lunar settlements, and how they compare to NASA's real-life plans for Artemis Base Camp.

articles.title: NASA still confident that Artemis astronauts will land on the moon in 2028 despite spacesuit delays
articles.description: NASA says it's confident that new spacesuits will be ready for a moon landing in 2028, even though a new report raises the possibility of a multiyear delay.

articles.title: Happy Earth Day! Celebrate with these amazing photos of our planet from space, from Apollo 8 to Artemis 2
articles.description: This Earth Day, we reflect on our home planet and look at Earth from space through history.

articles.title: Night and (Earth) Day
articles.description: This image, released in celebration of Earth Day, shows the terminator – the line between night and day – on Earth. The Artemis II astronauts captured this view on April 2, 2026, during their journey to the Moon. NASA science improves life on Earth every day. The agency provides insights on our home

articles.title: Meet the US Navy divers who welcomed the Artemis 2 astronauts home from the moon
articles.description: Four Navy divers made a contribution to the historic Artemis 2 moon mission, welcoming its astronauts home to Earth and making sure they were safe and healthy.

articles.title: Celebrate Earth Day with this Artemis 2 shot | Space photo of the day for April 22, 2026
articles.description: Celebrating Earth Day with an image of Earth captured by NASA's Artemis 2 mission around the moon.

articles.title: Artemis II Mission Milestones: An Image and Video Recap
articles.description: On April 1, 2026, Artemis II launched on a nearly 10-day voyage around the Moon, marking the first crewed flight of NASA’s Orion spacecraft. NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, and Christina Koch, along with CSA (Canadian Space Agency) astronaut Jeremy Hansen, splas

articles.title: NASA Invites Media to Jordan Artemis Accords Signing Ceremony
articles.description: The Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan will sign the Artemis Accords during a ceremony at 9:30 a.m. EDT Thursday, April 23, at NASA Headquarters in Washington. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman will host Ambassador Dina Kawar of the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan and U.S. Department of State Principal Deputy

articles.title: You've heard of the Space Force. Now there's a 'NASA Force' — but it's not about making space war
articles.description: NASA Force aims to recruit industry talent to the space agency for relatively short stints. Applications are open now in the wake of the Artemis 2 moon mission.

articles.title: NASA rolls out Artemis 3 SLS rocket's huge core stage to gear up for 2027 launch (photo)
articles.description: NASA rolled the Artemis 3 core stage out from the Michoud Assembly Facility in New Orleans on Monday (April 20). The next stop is the launch site: Kennedy Space Center in Florida.

articles.title: Artemis spacesuit development risks further delays
articles.description: New spacesuits for Artemis lunar missions and the International Space Station may not be ready until after the end of the decade, a report by NASA’s inspector general warns. The post Artemis spacesuit development risks further delays appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: Latvia joins the Artemis Accords
articles.description: Latvia is the latest country to sign the Artemis Accords as part of a new push to use the Accords to foster cooperation on NASA’s lunar exploration ambitions. The post Latvia joins the Artemis Accords appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: A peek at the far side of the moon | Space photo of the day for April 20, 2026
articles.description: The Artemis 2 astronauts captured a unique image of the moon's "dark side" during their historic April 2026 flyby.

articles.title: Space Force sets up ‘cislunar coordination’ office to focus beyond Earth orbit
articles.description: Officials say civil-military overlap with NASA grows as lunar activity accelerates The post Space Force sets up ‘cislunar coordination’ office to focus beyond Earth orbit appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: In the wake of Artemis 2, America needs to consider the ‘why’ of its government space program
articles.description: The moon itself does not wax and wane as frequently as American public opinion on its space program. The Artemis 2 astronauts barely gulped their first taste of fresh Pacific air before a chorus of voices began criticizing the Artemis program as a waste of money for which the American people receive

articles.title: Pragmata's tale of AI slop, humanity, & lunar conquest makes it the timeliest sci-fi game of the year
articles.description: Capcom's new IP is a welcome throwback to simpler action games, but its story and themes are shockingly well-timed and refreshingly optimistic.

articles.title: Artemis 2 and Tiangong space station astronauts set record for farthest distance between humans
articles.description: For a few moments on April 6, the four Artemis 2 moon astronauts and the three crewmates aboard China's Tiangong space station were farther away from each other than any humans had ever been.

articles.title: NASA’s Mobile Launcher Arrives at Vehicle Assembly Building
articles.description: After successfully being used to launch the Artemis II lunar test flight on April 1, NASA’s mobile launcher now is inside NASA Kennedy’s Vehicle Assembly Building (VAB) in Florida in preparation for the Artemis III test flight mission rocket stacking operations. articles.title: NASA’s Mobile Launcher Rolls Ahead of Artemis III Preparation
articles.description: Following the conclusion of NASA’s Artemis II test flight, teams at the agency’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida are shifting focus to Artemis III, which is targeted to launch next year, by rolling the mobile launcher from Launch Complex 39B to NASA’s Kennedy Vehicle Assembly Building (VAB) in Flori

articles.title: Ship 39 and Booster 19 Both Complete Full Engine Static Fires
articles.description: Following the successful return of Orion, attention for the Artemis program has now shifted to… The post Ship 39 and Booster 19 Both Complete Full Engine Static Fires appeared first on NASASpaceFlight.com.

articles.title: Artemis II Astronauts Back in Houston, Reunite with Families
articles.description: The Artemis II crew of NASA astronauts – Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, and Christina Koch, and CSA (Canadian Space Agency) astronaut Jeremy Hansen – has returned to the agency’s Johnson Space Center in Houston following their histo

articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 10: Live Re-Entry Updates
articles.description: Live re-entry updates for NASA’s Artemis II mission will be published on this page. All times are Eastern. 4:27 a.m. The Orion spacecraft is secured in the well deck of USS John P. Murtha after splashing down in the Pacific Ocean and carrying NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christi

articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 10: Crew Completes Final Burn Before Splashdown
articles.description: At 2:53 p.m. EDT, the Orion spacecraft ignited its thrusters for 8 seconds, producing a change in velocity of 4.2 feet-per-second and pushing Artemis II toward Earth. articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 10: Crew Sets for Final Burn, Splashdown
articles.description: The Artemis II crew — NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch and CSA (Canadian Space Agency) astronaut Jeremy Hansen — began the final phase of their journey home to the songs “Run to the Water” by Live, selected by the crew, and “Free” by Zac Brown Band, as they prepared for th

articles.title: Artemis II returns to Earth after historic 10-day mission around the Moon
articles.description: After 10 days, over 800,000 km traveled, and a historic flyby of the Moon, Artemis… The post Artemis II returns to Earth after historic 10-day mission around the Moon appeared first on NASASpaceFlight.com.

articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 9: Second Return Correction Burn Complete
articles.description: At 10:53 p.m. EDT, the Orion spacecraft ignited its thrusters for 9 seconds, producing an acceleration in velocity of 5.3 feet-per-second and pushing the Artemis II crew toward Earth. The crew is now more than halfway home.

articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 9: Crew Prepares to Come Home
articles.description: On their last full day in space, the Artemis II crew began the morning with “Lonesome Drifter” by Charley Crockett as they approached Earth at 147,337 miles.

articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 8: Crew Conducts Key Tests on Return to Earth
articles.description: Editor’s Note: This blog was updated on April 8, 2026, to update the time for the crew news conference. articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 7: First Return Correction Burn Complete
articles.description: At 8:03 p.m. EDT, the Orion spacecraft, named Integrity, ignited its thrusters for 15 seconds, producing a change in velocity of 1.6 feet-per-second and guiding the Artemis II crew toward Earth. NASA astronaut Christina Koch and CSA (C
